title: dendrophthoe rugulosa
intro: Dendrophthoe rugulosa, also known as the small-flowered mistletoe or woolly mistletoe, is a plant species that belongs to the family Loranthaceae. It is native to various regions, including China, Japan, and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Dendrophthoe rugulosa has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. It is often prepared as a tea or used in herbal formulations to treat various health conditions, such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, and is also used as a tonic to promote overall well-being.
